text.title: OpenAI Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-openai-sft` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.rationale: Bad JSONL silently fails or trains noise; pre-validation is the only cheap safety net.

rule.statement: Always supply a validation file (≥10 examples); OpenAI uses it for per-step eval traces.
rule.rationale: Without validation file the dashboard shows training loss only — no overfit signal.

rule.statement: epochs=3 default; bump to 5 for <300 examples; cap at 8 to avoid overfit.
rule.rationale: Below 300 examples need more passes; above 8 epochs typically overfits.

rule.statement: lr_multiplier=1.0 default; raise to 2.0 if loss plateaus, lower to 0.5 if loss oscillates.
rule.rationale: Default works for most cases; adjustments are diagnostic.

rule.statement: Poll job status with exponential backoff (5s → 60s capped); never tight-loop.
rule.rationale: Tight-loop polling burns quota and triggers rate limits.

rule.statement: Record returned model_id in version-controlled registry before any downstream use.
rule.rationale: Lost model_ids force re-train; registry prevents.
